news.title: When is next World Cup? FIFA Women's World Cup returns next year
news.lede: The 2026 FIFA World Cup is wrapping up with a Spain-Argentina final on July 19. It's the women's edition next year, as the 2027 FIFA Women's World Cup returns to the Southern Hemisphere. Fourteen countries have already qualified for the 2027 tournament. The remaining 18 slots will be filled before the official draw is announced later this year.

news.title: Are money and soft power draining World Cup football of its magic? | Richard Partington
news.lede: Fifa’s orchestration of incompatible or frankly absurd corporate sponsorship suggests the balance may have tipped too far. No more hydration breaks, no more obligatory pans of the TV cameras to Hollywood A-listers, or dread of the crushing inevitability of English disappointment. The Fifa World Cup 2026 is at an end, after another month of planet-straddling drama laced with significance and symbolism, both on and off the pitch. Before the kick-off between Spain and Argentina on Sunday evening, the result is already clear: football’s greatest prize is more than a sporting event; it is a geopolitically charged economic juggernaut.

news.title: Is the FIFA World Cup trophy made of gold? Everything to know
news.lede: One of the biggest flexes an athlete can display is hoisting up a championship trophy, parading around and sharing the moment with their family, friends and fans. And it doesn't get any better than the FIFA World Cup trophy. The World Cup trophy is one of the most luxurious, high-quality, opulent title trophies in all of sports. It's the only championship trophy that is made of real, pure gold.

news.title: Have Your Say: Will Italy fans support Spain or Argentina in 2026 World Cup Final?
news.lede: Spain and Argentina meet in the World Cup Final tonight, and there will be unpleasant implications for Italian football regardless of the outcome. So, who will you support? Spain and Argentina meet in New York for the 2026 World Cup Final tonight. The only two Serie A representatives remaining are both Argentines, Lautaro Martinez and Nico Paz, and neither is expected to start.

news.title: Who has most goals in World Cup history? Kylian Mbappé passes Lionel Messi with 2 goals in third-place game
news.lede: With the 2026 World Cup in full swing, it was only a matter of time before Argentina's Lionel Messi and France’s Kylian Mbappé broke the all-time record that stood before the tournament. Already considered by many as the greatest of all time, Messi was the first to score more goals than any other player in World Cup history. He earned his 17th and 18th during Argentina's 2-0 win over Austria. Then he added another against Jordan on a sublime free kick from outside the box for his 19th in the competition.

news.title: France at the World Cup 2026: Squad, fixtures, group and history
news.lede: Everything you need to know about France at the 2026 World Cup: their fixtures, squad, qualifying record, key players, coach and tournament history. France were runners-up last time but had to settle for fourth place at FIFA World Cup 2026. Didier Deschamps' side, led by captain Kylian Mbappé, topped Group I and beat Sweden, Paraguay and Morocco in the knockout phase before they were defeated by Spain in the semi-finals. They then lost a thriller against England in the play-off for third place.

news.title: 'Legend' or 'regrets'? A final that will define Lamine Yamal's World Cup
news.lede: Spain's Lamine Yamal is hoping to become just the third teenager to score in a World Cup final. When asked if Spain would win the World Cup, Lamine Yamal simply smiled and said "yes". His words to American broadcaster CBS in the build-up would have come as a surprise to no-one who has seen the confident teenager become one of the best players on the planet. For many though, for his prediction to come true, Lamine Yamal was going to have to set the tournament alight in his first ever World Cup.

news.title: Messi stands alone in World Cup scoring history. This chart shows how
news.lede: When Lionel Messi takes the pitch for the 2026 World Cup final against Spain at 3 p.m. ET on July 19 at MetLife Stadium in New Jersey, it might be his final international match for Argentina. But regardless of whether it is his final game or not, Messi will be far and away the all-time leader in goal contributions at the World Cup. USA TODAY analyzed goal and assist data from FIFA and Opta/Stats Perform to compile the 10 players with the most goal contributions in men’s World Cup history.
